topic: Parts and Schedules of the Constitution

questions.text: The Anti-Defection Law is contained in which Schedule of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Tenth Schedule, added by the 52nd Amendment (1985), lays down the grounds for disqualification of MPs/MLAs on account of defection.
questions.source: Appears repeatedly in SSC CGL-aligned test banks — exact year/paper not independently confirmed

questions.text: State land reform laws were protected from judicial review by being placed under which Schedule?
questions.explanation: The Ninth Schedule was added by the 1st Constitutional Amendment, 1951, specifically to shield certain land reform laws from being struck down for violating Fundamental Rights.
questions.source: Appears repeatedly in SSC CGL-aligned test banks — exact year/paper not independently confirmed

questions.text: The Union List, State List, and Concurrent List are mentioned in which Schedule of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Seventh Schedule divides legislative subjects into the Union List (Centre), State List (States), and Concurrent List (both).
questions.source: Appears repeatedly in SSC CGL-aligned test banks — exact year/paper not independently confirmed

questions.text: Which Schedule of the Constitution deals specifically with the administration of Tribal Areas in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram?
questions.explanation: The Sixth Schedule covers tribal area administration specifically in these four northeastern states with greater autonomy, distinct from the more general Fifth Schedule.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — anticipated based on the common 5th-vs-6th Schedule confusion trap

questions.text: Which Part of the Constitution was added by the 97th Amendment, 2011, dealing with Co-operative Societies?
questions.explanation: Part IXB was inserted by the 97th Amendment, 2011, giving citizens the right to form co-operative societies.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — anticipated based on the 'lettered Parts are later insertions' pattern

questions.text: How many languages are currently listed in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution, and how many were there originally?
questions.explanation: The Eighth Schedule started with 14 languages and now lists 22, with additions made by the 21st (1967), 71st (1992), and 92nd (2003) Amendments.
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — frequently underrated because students assume the count never changed

questions.text: Which Schedule allocates the number of Rajya Sabha seats to each State and Union Territory?
questions.explanation: The Fourth Schedule deals specifically with the allocation of seats in the Rajya Sabha (Council of States).
questions.source: Not an official PYQ — an easy-to-overlook Schedule since it's less famous than the 7th, 9th, or 10th

questions.text: Which Amendment added the Eleventh and Twelfth Schedules to the Constitution, and what do they cover?
options: 73rd & 74th Amendments — Powers of Panchayats and Municipalities
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added the Eleventh Schedule (29 Panchayat subjects) and the 74th Amendment (1992) added the Twelfth Schedule (18 Municipality subjects).
questions.source: Pattern frequently recurring across SSC CGL/CHSL polity papers in various phrasings
